item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently reject the statement from the first turn through the final turn. Agent A grounds opposition in Indonesian concerns about public-interest claims being used to suppress criticism or minorities; Agent B grounds opposition in American civil-liberties concerns. Both acknowledge that public health, safety, order, or social interests can matter, but only under strict limits, so these are limited concessions rather than position shifts. No visible flip.
